zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fife於1290年出生在蘇格蘭費伏時，他的父親Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewyk25歲，母親Mrs Symond Coupre20歲。他和Mrs Symon Cooper至少有1個兒子。他去世於1350年享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fife于1290年出生在苏格兰費伏时，他的父亲Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewyk25岁，母亲Mrs Symond Coupre20岁。他和Mrs Symon Cooper至少有1个儿子。他去世于1350年享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cooper
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker and repairer of wooden vessels such as barrels, tubs, buckets, casks, and vats, from Middle English couper, cowper (apparently from Middle Dutch kūper, a derivative of kūp ‘tub, container’, which was borrowed independently into English as coop). The prevalence of the surname, its cognates, and equivalents bears witness to the fact that this was one of the chief specialist trades in the Middle Ages throughout Europe. In North America, the English surname has absorbed some cases of like-sounding cognates from other languages, for example Dutch Kuiper .
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Kupfer and Kupper (see Kuper ).
Dutch: occupational name for a buyer or merchant, Middle Dutch coper.

variants.name: Kuiper
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Hendrik, Willem, Harm, Klaas, Kort, Marinus, Nicolaas, Wouter.
Dutch: occupational name for a cooper, Middle Dutch cup(e)re.

variants.name: Couper
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Cooper  .

variants.name: Hooper
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name for a cooper, someone who fitted wooden or metal hoops on wooden casks and barrels, or a barrel-maker, from Middle English hoper, an agent derivative of hop ‘hoop, band’. Compare Cooper .

variants.name: Coop
meanings: English (Lancashire): metonymic occupational name for a cooper, from Middle English coupe, cupe ‘tub, cask’ (from Latin cūpa), probably used for a cooper; compare Cooper . In some cases the surname may have been derived from a pub or house sign.
Americanized form of German or Dutch   Koop  .
History: It was David Koop (his father's surname also spelled Kupe) from Germany whose surname was changed to Coop after he settled in PA toward the end of the 18th century. He later moved to VA.

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variants.name: Earle
meanings: English: variant of   Earl  .

variants.name: Harle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Johann, Kurt.
South German (Härle): nickname from a diminutive of Middle High German hār ‘hair’.

English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from Kirkharle and Little Harle in Northumberland (earlier simply Herle, Harle), possibly named from an Old English personal name Herela (a derivative of various compound names with the first element here ‘army’) + Old English lēah ‘wood, clearing’.

variants.name: Earls
meanings: English: variant of Earl with genitival -s, denoting someone who was a member of an earl's household, or, with post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland, when this is not the English name, it is from Gaelic Mac an Iarla ‘son of the earl’.

variants.name: Hearl
meanings: English: variant of Earl with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Count
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earll
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Earl  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Fife
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the province and former kingdom of Fife in East Scotland, a name of obscure etymology. Tradition has it that the name is derived from an eponymous Fib, one of the seven sons of Cruithne, legendary founding father of the Picts.

variants.name: Fyffe
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Fife  .

variants.name: Fyfe
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Fife  .

nameMeanings.name: Symon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fife was born in 1290, in Fife, Scotland, his father, Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewyk, was 25 and his mother, Mrs Symond Coupre, was 20. He had at least 1 son with Mrs Symon Cooper. He died in 1350, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fife est né en 1290, à Fife, Écosse, son père, Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewyk, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs Symond Coupre, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs Symon Cooper. Il est décédé en 1350, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fife 1290 in Fife, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewyk 25 und seine Mutter Mrs Symond Coupre 20. Er hatte mit Mrs Symon Cooper mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1350 im Alter von 60 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fife è nato il nel 1290 nel Fife, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewyk, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mrs Symond Coupre, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs Symon Cooper. Morì nel 1350, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.Fife, Scotland: スコットランドフィフ
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fifeが1290年にスコットランドフィフで生まれた時，父Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewykは25歳，母Mrs Symond Coupreは20歳であった。彼はMrs Symon Cooperとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1350年に60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fife(이)가 1290년 스컷랜드 피프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewyk(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Symond Coupre(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Mrs Symon Cooper(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1350년 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fife nasceu em 1290, em Fife, Scotland, seu pai, Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewyk, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Symond Coupre, tinha 20 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs Symon Cooper. Ele faleceu em 1350, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fife родился в 1290 г. в местности фиф, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewyk было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Symond Coupre, 20. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs Symon Cooper. Он умер в 1350 г. в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Symon Cooper Earl of Fife nació en 1290, en Fife, Scotland, su papá, Sir Symond Coupre Del Counte de Berewyk, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mrs Symond Coupre, tenía 20 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs Symon Cooper. Murió en 1350, a la edad de 60 años.
